title: The Implementation of Rewind in Braid
creator: Jonathan Blow
description: A technical GDC lecture on storing, reconstructing, and replaying Braid's world state so that players can rewind long stretches of play accurately within console memory limits.
useful: It connects a defining game mechanic to concrete engineering constraints and shows how implementation choices determine which higher-level design ideas remain possible.

manualLinkStatus.note: GDC Vault session page opened successfully during editorial review.

audience: Gameplay programmers and technical designers working on deterministic simulation, replay, rollback, or mechanics built around historical state.
learning: Separate reversible state from effects that can be reconstructed
Budget a long gameplay history within strict memory limits
Relate engine architecture to the design space of a mechanic

editorial.note: Verified against the GDC Vault session record.
